Operation 'prod_tt_sasportal.nodes.devices.createSigned' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description lacks a summary entirely and provides minimal context. Enhanced version clarifies the cryptographic signing aspect, specifies what inputs are expected (device configuration), what is returned (SasPortalDevice with signature metadata), and the side effect (establishes device identity). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus standard device creation.

data.current_description: Creates a signed device under a node or customer.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a signed device under a node or customer
data.description_suggestion: Creates a cryptographically signed device resource under a specified node or customer hierarchy. The operation accepts device configuration details and generates a signed device certificate. Returns the created SasPortalDevice object with signature validation metadata. This operation establishes a new device identity in the SAS Portal system and enables device authentication.
